cleaned_sections.strain_interpretation: Strain This indicator measures how you feel about your environment. It relates to how safe, satisfied and connected you feel to your community and neighbourhood. It also measures whether you experience your home and neighbourhood to be conducive to the quality of your life. The extent to which you live your life indoors will impact on your strain score in this area. This is a subjective measure and does not indicate that you are safe or unsafe; it merely taps into whether you feel safe or unsafe. It therefore does not provide an objective measure of the quality of your environment. Corinne your perceived level of Environmental strain is LOW. ● You feel connected and quite positive about your environment and community despite your collective challenges ● You feel safe in your neighbourhood and home ● You are satisfied with how much time you spend outdoors or in your broader community ● Experience your home environment as comfortable and conducive to getting things done ● You are not distracted from your core focus by environmental and service delivery issues in your community Vocational Strain This indicator measures how you are feeling about your career choices and work. It relates to how passionate you are about your work and how enjoyable it is to you at present. Your score will also be impacted by the amount of stress you experience at work and the quality of your relationships at work. If you are unemployed or have made alternative vocational choices at present, this will also impact on your strain score in this area. This is a subjective measure and does not indicate performance at work. It merely taps into your perceptions about your current vocational reality. Corinne your perceived level of Vocational strain is HIGH. ● You are quite dissatisfied with some aspects of your career or vocational choices ● There are aspects of your work that gets to you. You may not be enjoying work much at present ● You don’t get along well with a lot of people at work ● You carry a huge amount of responsibility at work and that this may be somewhat unsustainable in the demands and pressure it places on you ● You are demotivated or disengaged at work or in the process of disengaging ● You wish you were somewhere else or working somewhere else when you are at work [Page 21] Physical Strain This indicator measures how you are feeling about your connection to your body and physicality. It relates to how healthy, energised and fit you feel. This is a subjective measure and may not indicate that you are healthy or unhealthy, it merely taps into whether you feel healthy or unhealthy. Corinne your perceived level of Physical strain is MEDIUM. ● You feel somewhat positive or neutral about your health at present ● You are comfortable with your body and weight but there may be some things you don’t quite like or want to accept ● You may want to improve your fitness levels and spend a bit more time exercising than you’re able to ● You don’t get ill that o�en, but do on occasion struggle with some minor health issues ● You wake up feeling more ready and energised on some days than on others ● Your energy levels fluctuate throughout your average day Interpersonal Strain This indicator measures how you are feeling about your intimate relationships with family and friends. It relates to the quality of these relationships and whether you are able to invest in them. Perceived lack of support and high levels of conflict in close relationships will impact on your strain score in this area. This is a subjective measure and may not indicate whether these relationships are healthy or unhealthy. It merely taps into your experience of the quality of your intimate relationships. Corinne your perceived level of Interpersonal strain is LOW. ● You are satisfied with the amount of time you get to spend with friends and family ● You have a social life that meets your needs ● You experience a low or normal amount of conflict in your close relationships ● You are satisfied with the level of intimacy in your closest relationships ● You feel supported by the people close to you [Page 22] Psychological Strain This indicator measures your perception of how well you are coping with your present circumstances on a psychological level. This is definitely not a tool aimed at any clinical diagnosis whatsoever. It is not an indicator of psychological problems but a high score may warrant further professional investigation and support into the impact of your circumstances on your emotional wellbeing This is a subjective measure and may not indicate that you are actually coping or not coping, it merely taps into whether you perceive yourself as able to cope with your challenges on a psychological and emotional level and the extent to which you feel overwhelmed. Corinne your perceived level of Psychological strain is HIGH. ● You are unsure of your ability to emotionally cope with aspects of your present circumstances ● You are affected by your emotions to the extent that you feel overwhelmed at times and this is likely to be impacting on your wellbeing ● You are anxious and tense most of the time ● You feel the need to "numb" or "tune out" from the intensity of your feelings or circumstances on occasion ● You may feel down or even depressed quite o�en ● You are likely to want to improve your coping mechanisms but may not be sure of how to do this Please note that if your score is very high in this area and you feel overwhelmed at present, it is strongly advised that you speak to someone in a professional capacity like a counsellor, medical practitioner or psychologist about these feelings. Happiness This indicator approaches strain from a more generalised and positive perspective. It measures whether you feel optimistic, joyful and happy about your life at present. It therefore relates to your subjective experience of fulfilment and joy in your life but other people may not experience that way in which you project yourself in quite the same way. This merely taps into how you feel about your life and not how others perceive you. Corinne your perceived level of Happiness strain is LOW. ● General circumstances in your life are detracting from your ability to feel happy, excited and hopeful about things ● You are more of a realist or pessimist than an optimist ● You would like to experience more joy and fulfilment from day-to-day ● There are a lot of things you wish you could change about your life ● You have more stress than joy in your life at present

cleaned_sections.decision_framework: The capacity to make effective, well-considered decisions is a critical competency in a professional environment. Effective decisions involve three phases or types of activity, namely: EXPERIENCE: Actual experience, data scanning and data collection E S A INTELLIGIBILITY: Interpretation and making sense of experience or data H P COMMITMENT: Applying judgement, making the decision and taking actions Your decision style is not only strongly impacted by your dominant Center of Intelligence but also by your main Ennea type and the amount of strain you are experiencing at present. Effective and wise decisions are made when all three Centers of Intelligence are positively engaged in the decision making process. Decisions happen in context. The more you are able to consider and position the context within which you need to make decisions, the more effective your decisions become. Feeling Centered Decisions Corinne your dominant Center of Expression is Feeling Centered, and is likely to impact on your decision making in the following ways: ● Able to tune into your own feelings, needs and resistance to decisions ● Able to tune in and listen to feelings, needs and resistance of others to decisions ● Strong awareness of impact of decisions on people and yourself ● Decisions draw on past ● Quality of decisions impacted by your emotional state on the day Your Feeling Center is likely to lead you to emphasise the Experience Phase of decision making as you are likely to be quite tuned into and responsive to your environment on an emotive level. The drawbacks of this approach to decision making include: ● Getting stuck in emotions (Emotional Paralysis) ● Subjective decision making ● Desire to accommodate feelings of others make tough and unpopular decisions difficult to make ● Over-emphasis of relationships, under-emphasis of planning ● Sentimental decisions strongly based on historical data ● Trouble sticking to decisions as the environment and people within it change [Page 33] Impact of your Ennea 4 style when you make decisions:   You may over-emphasise personal experience When analysing information before making a and values in the decision making process which may decision, this information is likely to not only include lead you to at times lose sight of the bigger picture tangible information but is likely to have quite strong opinions  about your decisions and willing to passionately You may need to develop your ability to be defend your decisions when necessary but need to be moderate and take the middle ground when making aware that your tenacity may make it difficult for you decisions, as you may err on the side of extremes at to enrol people in your decision times, either being excessively decision projecting your own emotions onto others making, you may need to pay attention to your listening skills and receptiveness to their contributions, especially if their inputs differ from yours.  Despite potentially being quite analytical about decisions, your decision making style may favour subjective analysis over objective analysis and should guard against over-emphasising intuition in particularly strong negative reaction for you [Page 34] High Strain Corinne, the high level of strain you are experiencing at present is likely to impact on your decision making in the following ways: ● More likely to rely on your Limbic System when making decisions, thereby impacting on your capacity to access your rational and objective faculties in your Prefrontal Cortex ● More anxious and susceptible to the pressure surrounding the decision, while also impacting on your perception of the relative importance of a decision ● Make you potentially more reactive and defensive when decisions are challenged ● Short term focus, immediate results, crisis decisions ● Exhaustion and depleted energy reserves are likely to impact negatively on the quality of your decisions ● Premature closure when analysing information or consulting others ● Limits flexibility and scope of exploration
cleaned_sections.strategic_leadership: Strategic leadership is the capacity to relate, align and maximise the internal direction of the organisation to the external world and the macro-environmental factors impacting on the organisation and its success. Corinne, as an Ennea 4, your strategic leadership is likely to be influenced by your core drivers and motivations in the following ways: Visioning You are likely to emphasise the development of an inspiring, clear vision for your team, business unit or organisation as a key priority. In fact, a vision may not be sufficient in terms of creating meaning. Cra�ing a clear purpose statement may therefore supplant the need to have a vision in place. The vision or purpose you cra� is likely to be both creative and meaningful, but may be quite elaborate, abstract, emotive and complicated. You therefore need to apply the litmus test to the vision by asking whether it is clear to others in the team and whether it is going to move the team forward. If not, you may need to simplify and make the vision more concrete, while keeping in mind that the vision is only as good as the action it inspires. Once the vision is in place, you may run the risk of assuming that the rest will fall in place. You need to recognise that not everybody may be as compelled by the vision as you are. Similarly, not everybody likes the freedom to follow their own way of doing things and need more concrete structure, tactical and operational guidance. Strategic Thinking You are likely to enjoy the complexity and abstract nature of strategic thinking. Emergent strategy processes may be particularly appealing to you, as you value intuition. The quality of your application in this area may however be largely determined by your level of interest in the different aspects of the business. You are likely to develop an in-depth knowledge of the business aspects you consider as important, while not paying attention to those aspects of the business you value as being less significant. This will impact on the extent to which you are able to apply yourself strategically within the context of the business as a whole. When considering the strategy, you may be quite dismissive of the status quo and strategies that are likely to increase the bureaucracy within the system. Your emphasis on innovation and creativity may lead you to push change enthusiastically, at times without appreciation for what has worked well up to now. You may also find that you get stuck in your thinking process when you feel that there is something missing or something that has been overlooked in a strategy, especially if you can’t put your finger on what exactly that may be. [Page 38] Alignment Your focus within the alignment sphere is to ensure that your actions and that of the team are aligned to purpose and vision. Once people derive a sense of meaning and are inspired by what they do, you may feel that a major part of the alignment job has been taken care of. Aligning with your inner compass, may however be far from sufficient when translating strategy into practical action. More concrete, operational and tactical alignment and action plans may require your attention. Change Management You may find that you enjoy leading large scale change projects. When leading change that is about something that has meaning and importance to you, you are therefore likely to embrace the possibilities of the project. The key to your ability to act as a change agent, is that you need to strongly believe in the change yourself, before being able to commit to leadership responsibilities in relation to the change. DEVELOPMENT EXERCISE: What to focus on as a strategic leader  Translate strategy into clear tactical and  Ensure that strategies are balanced and operational action steps include all elements that are important to the business, not just those that are important to you  Focus more on implementation and outputs – it is not all about the process  Don’t take setbacks, failures and resistance to change personally  Appreciate what already works well more, and make an effort to distinguish between the need to change and blanket assumptions that change and revolution is necessary
cleaned_sections.team_dynamics: Within a team environment, your Enneagram type is likely to influence not only how you interact with others, but also what you view as ideal team relationships and behaviours. Teams go through different phases of development. Tuckman’s four stages of team development, namely Forming-Storming-Norming-Performing, illustrate the process through which teams go as it becomes more effective over time. Each of these phases are necessary if a team wants to grow towards high performance. Ideally teams move through the first three phases rapidly, spending most time in the Performing phase. In reality, some teams get stuck and struggle to move beyond the Forming and Storming phases. Your Impact on Team Corinne, to understand the impact you have on your team, you will need to consider the phase of team development your team is currently in. You may find that different teams in different areas of your life are in different stages, which will impact on the way you show up in each group. The different Ennea types also differ in terms of: ● Types of goals that resonate with you ● Level of interdependence that you are most comfortable with ● Typical role played in the team As an Ennea 4, you prefer goals that are: ● Meaningful rather than mundane or contrived ● Challenging and that provide you with opportunities to express yourself ● Broad in scope but able to break down into smaller, manageable goals or activities ● Benchmarked Interdependence and Team Role Corinne, as you like to express yourself in your own unique way, you prefer team environments that still allow self-expression. Even when the task requires interdependence, you prefer enough space within the interdependencies to still act in a more independent manner. You may therefore feel somewhat ambivalent about high levels of interdependence. At the same time you may also find that you are quite sensitive to being le� out of team discussions, activities or decisions. [Page 40] FORMING: NORMING: This is the first stage of team development. This phase Once individuals start resolving their differences and is o�en characterised by a high level of pseudo come to a shared understanding of the way in which to behaviour, as individuals avoid conflict and do things, the team moves to the third stage of controversy in favour of getting to know each other, development. The norming phase enables the team to being accepted and fitting in. During this stage establish norms for working together, a common goal individuals gather impressions of each other while and preferred level of interdependence to ensure that attending to routine tasks as the team goes about results are achieved and problems are solved. Team this stage, teams take mutual responsibility for goals members may behave quite independently at this and are characterised by a shared desire to address and point in time. At this stage of development most resolve challenges. Team members are able to individuals put forward their best behaviour and acknowledge that the task at hand cannot be avoid conflict. As a result, this can be quite a accomplished without the input of the others. comfortable phase during which people get to know each other. PERFORMING: STORMING: Although not all teams reach the performing stage, high performance teams are interdependent units with a This is the second stage of team development and is shared sense of purpose. These teams are o�en self- normally characterised by high levels of conflict. directed, requiring little external supervision as team During this phase, different ideas and perspectives members hold each other mutually accountable. start competing with each other for attention. Team Dissent and transparency enable the team to work members start opening up to each other and are more through challenges in a candid manner that allows for likely to confront and challenge others. This enables correction, learning and support. Even the most high- the team to uncover contentious issues, problems performing teams cycle through the stages of team and differences. It may however also lead to development as circumstances change and different protection of interests and positions, win-lose challenges present themselves. scenarios, competitive behaviour, blaming and the formation of factions or silos. Some teams work through this phase quickly, while other teams may get stuck in storming for extended periods of time. [Page 41] Your Ennea Type and Team Stages FORMING Corinne, during the forming stage of team development, others are likely to experience you as: ● Somewhat internally focused ● Not consistently connecting to the group, remaining somewhat separate or peripheral to the attempts to form and establish the team and the team's identity ● More attuned to emotions than to the task needs of the situation ● 'I' rather than 'we' oriented ● Be somewhat uncomfortable with the "pseudo" behaviour and niceties that o�en form part of interactions during this phase of team development STORMING As an Ennea 4, you may find yourself feeling quite comfortable during the storming phase of team development and are likely to: ● Prefer the authenticity of expression to the more polite expression that is typical during the Forming Stage ● Be comfortable as long as people don't become personally attacking or hurtful ● If you become the focus of negative attention in the group, you may feel an overwhelm of emotions, leading you to either explode or withdraw ● Be patient in wanting issues fully explored and resolved before moving to the next matter at hand NORMING You may not always be completely at ease in teams that are at this stage of team development, and will: ● Resist norms and rules that may inhibit your individual expression and that may "box" your creativity in any way ● Be more supportive of rules that provide a balance between freedom and clarity of expectations from a role and process perspective ● May be less vocal in driving the agenda for the team, as long as the process and outcomes are authentic and meaningful ● Feel somewhat ambivalent about some aspects of interdependence and team identity PERFORMING You should be quite comfortable in teams that are able to reach this stage of team development, and will therefore be: ● Willing to contribute and work hard towards common goals as long as they are meaningful ● Less likely to marginalise yourself in relation to the team but still unlikely to take your identity from the team ● Able to make a contribution to the team that showcases your unique talent and contribution ● More able to concentrate on task matters ● Still somewhat inept at relishing success fully, being quite sensitive to what is still going wrong or missing in the team
cleaned_sections.coaching_relationship: This section of the report is aimed at increasing your awareness of how you may be showing up in a coaching relationship. If you are being coached at present, this may be useful to discuss with your coach. If you are not in a formal coaching relationship, you may want to relate this to your behaviour when being coached informally at work or by your manager, or alternatively to reflect on past experiences of mentoring, coaching and any other significant development relationships. Corinne, as an Ennea 4, you are likely to want to utilise coaching to create opportunities to make sense of your world in depth. See these moments as great opportunities to examine the way in which your emotional landscape may dominate your expression of your identity at times. Rather than reacting to the emotions in the moment, you may want to use the coaching situation to observe the process, your emotions and reaction to these emotions with your coach to create some distance between emotion and self.

Rather than without becoming consumed by them. The ability feeling guilty or conflicted about your desires, to observe your emotions from a healthy distance embrace them as a legitimate part of who you are. will help you see the bigger picture and gain a more grounded perspective. [Page 13] Thinking Center of Expression: MEDIUM ● Thinking Energy is COOL. ● It relates to logic, analysis and rational processing of information as well as criticism and mental chatter. ● It corresponds with the area of your brain known as the prefrontal cortex which is sometimes referred to as the executive function of the brain because of its capacity for prioritisation, judgement, goals-setting and analysis. ● Unhealthy expression of this Center is like the busy, “monkey mind”. ● Healthy expression of this Center is quiet and spacious. Action Center of Expression: LOW ● Action Energy is HOT. ● It impatiently drives action and results and is mostly experienced as gut instinct and bodily presence. ● It corresponds with the area of your brain known as the brainstem which plays an important role in conducting sensory information and the basic bodily functions that keep you alive. ● Unhealthy expression of this Center is unfocused and manic. ● Healthy expression of this Center is grounded and alive. As your Action Center is low in its expression, others are likely to experience you as: ● Responding to challenges at your own pace ● Less likely to trust and act on gut response or information from the body when making decisions or confronted by an issue ● Easily falling into habitual thinking, feeling and action patterns as a result of insufficient contact with body intelligence. DEVELOPMENT EXERCISE: The Center of Intelligence Here are some ideas on practices and approaches that support the expression of all The Centers in an intelligent, present and mindful way. As your Feeling Center is most likely to be over-expressed, you can support a more balanced and healthy expression of this Center through the following exercises: ● Build self-awareness regarding the distinction between feeling energy that is receptive and authentic vs. feeling energy that is reactive and oversensitive. ● Tune into the heart as a regular practice by simply naming the emotions you are experiencing at a given point in time. This acknowledges emotions while also allowing you to move from feeling energy into the other Centers. ● Focus on becoming more awake and attuned to The Centers of expression that are not as dominant, namely Action and Thinking. Create both emotional and forward momentum on issues by asking yourself what you know and think about the issue at hand. ● Request feedback from others on whether your emotions are “leaking” into your environment in an unhealthy way. For example, a�er a meeting check in with a colleague you trust on whether the expression or projection of your emotions during the meeting had an impact on the pace, quality and participation of others. ● Practice giving a voice to your feelings and heart through the application of singing, dancing, storytelling, sweet silence or any creative practice that enables you to externalise and express feelings without becoming focused on a product or output. ● Participate in a group where compassion and open-heartedness are practiced. [Page 17]   As you struggle to create objective distance You may find the self-development journey between yourself and your feelings, your highly particularly difficult and challenging. It may not be easy subjective view of the world may keep you trapped in for you to commit to and embrace the vulnerability that your feelings and in patterns of relating that impact comes with the territory of personal growth and getting negatively on your self-esteem, relationships and over yourself. productivity. Ennea 4 links to you will also develop your capacity for affirming Ennea 1 which urges you to take pleasure in routine, others. ordinary tasks. Ennea 4 links to Ennea 2 which urges you to focus on others and not just on yourself. This  Choose to stay connected rather than focus on others, enables you to be more aware and isolating yourself when things are not going well. sensitive to how others are experiencing interactions This requires you to reach out to others in a way as well as their needs and emotions. that enables them to understand and meet you, rather than overwhelming them with your emotions, intensity and anger.  Become aware of your tendency to personalise and incorporate negative data and experiences. This is known as introjections and is likely to lead you to be hyper-alert to clues that others may be upset with you. This hyper- sensitivity does not only impact on relationships and your feelings immediately, but can also create a reserve of negativity that surfaces time and again. By working to release and dissolve this reservoir, you create space within yourself for greater objectivity and balance. [Page 19] DEVELOPMENT EXERCISE The bottom line is that if your strain level is high, especially if it has been high for a while, you need to do something about it. Consider the following development activities:  Connect to hope - Believing that things can  Have a big dream - What is the possibility in and will improve is an important first step. [Page 31] What you do when triggered ● You are likely to experience strong emotions and may find yourself over-identifying with these feelings ● Your emotions may be conflicting and quite durable, tending not to fade away quickly ● You may find yourself going quiet and retreating into a more private space to make sense of things ● When the situation is really important or distressing, you may find yourself insisting that it be addressed and discussed the moment you bring it to the attention of others ● You are likely to look to others for support and understanding but may behave in a way that makes it difficult for others to satisfy this need ● You may find the expression of your emotions in conflict situations both essential and cathartic ● You will want to dwell on matters to not only make sure you are understood but to also gain perspectives and explore alternatives What others should do When you are triggered, agitated or in the middle of a conflict situation, others may want to consider the following to diffuse the situation and create a constructive environment where resolution becomes possible: ● As an Ennea 4, you want others to validate and understand your emotions. This makes it important for others to listen to you and to give you the space to vent and express yourself. ● An invitation to listen and the patience to listen fully and empathically, rather than cutting you off therefore aid the resolution of conflict ● Suggestions that you “calm down”, “stop overreacting” or insinuations that you are being unreasonable and sensitive are therefore likely to incense rather than calm the situation and should be avoided as far as possible ● Others can use paraphrasing to show that they have understood and heard you ● Others should take care not to act in a way that may be interpreted as blaming or accusing ● Affirmation and positive conclusion to discussions are likely to assist you to move towards more positive emotions and feelings, as ending things on a negative note may lead to escalation of doubts, insecurities and other constraining feelings over the short to medium term ● Others need to be willing to explore issues in depth, as keeping it at a surface level may not feel satisfy your need as an Ennea 4. DEVELOPMENT EXERCISE Self-Regulation If you want to manage yourself and your triggers in a sensible and constructive way, the following may be useful when engaging with others and building relationships: ● Create cognitive distance between your emotions and who you are ● Be sensitive to how your timing sits with others, as forcing the issue onto an agenda when the timing is wrong for others, may escalate issues unnecessarily ● Actively inquire into the perspective of the other person, rather than just dumping your perspective on them ● Listen as much as you want to be listened to ● Don’t take yourself and your emotions so seriously, keeping in mind that the interpretation you have construed is just your story and not necessarily the objective truth ● Breathe deeply and move slowly rather than allowing the flood of emotions to rush you along ● Try to stick with what is relevant to this issue, at this point in time rather than delving excessively into past issues and previous conflicts ● Use exercise to keep you moving forward on issues ● Try to recognise the relationship between your anger and feelings of sadness or depression. Your depressed feelings may be a manifestation of introjected anger. ● Remind yourself that your growth depends on how you react and not on the reaction of others to you [Page 36] Delegation When a task interests you, you may struggle to delegate it and may miss opportunities to utilise and involve others. When delegating routine tasks, you may be quite dismissive of these tasks. Remember that if you devalue the task, individuals may feel equally devalued when you ask them to do it. When delegating you may find that you emphasise creative ideas and suggestions more than practical guidelines. This may work well at high levels of task maturity but may be somewhat open-ended, thereby creating problems in execution for individuals who are still in the process of mastering the task at hand. Performance Management As you have very particular personal ideas about quality, aesthetics, originality and meaning, others may find that you are difficult to please. In the performance management setting, you are likely to come across as critical and even condescending when unhappy with performance. You may also take any disagreement with your performance ratings personally, and may overreact to such challenges. Others may find performance discussions to be intense, personal experience and the depth to which you explore issues may not be comfortable to all the individuals in your team. Motivation As having a sense of meaning and purpose is important to you, you will try to instil this in the team as well. By painting a compelling vision for others and trying to put emotions into words, you may however find that you struggle to clearly articulate this. This may lead to highly metaphoric language or multiple attempts at rephrasing your message. Others may find this confusing. When your emotions and moods interfere with your leadership of the team, you may also affect others negatively as your emotions are imposed on or infuses the team. DEVELOPMENT EXERCISE: Top priorities in managing teams for results  Be clear in your management  Don't take setbacks, failures and communication disagreements too personally. Develop filters when processing and receiving information about yourself  Provide individuals with the structure, as a manager and leader guidance and practical guidelines towards task completion that they need, rather than  Balance your awareness of your own needs withholding that based on your own need for with a greater awareness of the needs of the rest of freedom your team  Attend to the tasks you dislike most or delegate them without being dismissive or demeaning about the task [Page 38] Alignment Your focus within the alignment sphere is to ensure that your actions and that of the team are aligned to purpose and vision. Once people derive a sense of meaning and are inspired by what they do, you may feel that a major part of the alignment job has been taken care of. Aligning with your inner compass, may however be far from sufficient when translating strategy into practical action. More concrete, operational and tactical alignment and action plans may require your attention. Change Management You may find that you enjoy leading large scale change projects. When leading change that is about something that has meaning and importance to you, you are therefore likely to embrace the possibilities of the project. The key to your ability to act as a change agent, is that you need to strongly believe in the change yourself, before being able to commit to leadership responsibilities in relation to the change. DEVELOPMENT EXERCISE: What to focus on as a strategic leader  Translate strategy into clear tactical and  Ensure that strategies are balanced and operational action steps include all elements that are important to the business, not just those that are important to you  Focus more on implementation and outputs – it is not all about the process  Don’t take setbacks, failures and resistance to change personally  Appreciate what already works well more, and make an effort to distinguish between the need to change and blanket assumptions that change and revolution is necessary
